entry.title: Atlas App Services
entry.description: Atlas App Services provides backend services like authentication, sync, and serverless functions for MongoDB Atlas applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1,000,000 monthly function invocations and 10GB data transfer.
caveats: Requires MongoDB Atlas cluster (free tier available)
Some advanced features require paid plans
billingRiskNotes: Usage above free tier may incur charges

useCases: Sync data between devices and cloud
quickstartSteps: Sign up for MongoDB Atlas and create a free cluster.
Navigate to App Services in the Atlas dashboard.
Create a new App Service and configure authentication.
Define functions or sync rules as needed.
Integrate the SDK into your application.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need managed backend services like authentication, sync, or serverless functions tightly integrated with MongoDB Atlas.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require backend services for non-MongoDB databases or need advanced customization beyond provided features.
entry.body: Atlas App Services offers managed backend features like authentication, sync, and serverless functions, making it easy to build applications on MongoDB Atlas.

entry.title: Azure App Service
entry.description: Azure App Service enables fast deployment and scalable hosting for web apps and APIs with integrated DevOps and security features.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1 GB storage and 60 minutes compute per day for web, mobile, or API apps.

useCases: Deploying personal or demo web apps
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create a new App Service resource.
Select the Free (F1) tier during setup.
Deploy your web app using GitHub, local Git, or ZIP upload.
Browse to the provided URL to verify deployment.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to quickly deploy and test web apps or APIs with minimal configuration and no upfront cost.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for production workloads needing high availability, custom domains with SSL, or advanced scaling and networking features.
entry.body: Azure App Service provides a managed platform for deploying web apps and APIs with integrated scaling, security, and DevOps support. The free tier is ideal for learning, prototyping, and low-traffic demos, but lacks production-grade features such as custom domain SSL and guaranteed uptime.

entry.title: Azure Static Web Apps
entry.description: Azure Static Web Apps offers fast, secure hosting for static sites with integrated CI/CD and serverless APIs.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes hosting for static sites with custom domains, SSL, and serverless APIs.
caveats: Limited to 1 custom domain on free tier
No custom authentication providers on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create a new Static Web App resource.
Connect your GitHub repository containing your static site.
Configure build settings and deployment branch.
Review and create the Static Web App.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need fast, secure static site hosting with built-in CI/CD and simple serverless API integration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require dynamic server-side rendering, advanced authentication, or complex backend integrations.
entry.body: Azure Static Web Apps provides free static site hosting with integrated CI/CD, custom domains, SSL, and serverless API support, ideal for personal or small team projects.

entry.title: Cloudflare Pages
entry.description: Cloudflare Pages offers fast, secure, and scalable static site hosting with built-in CI/CD and global CDN.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes unlimited sites, automatic deployments, and global CDN for static assets.
caveats: Build minutes and concurrent builds are limited on free tier.
No server-side rendering or dynamic backends on free plan.

quickstartSteps: Sign up or log in to Cloudflare.
Connect your GitHub or GitLab repository.
Configure build settings and output directory.
Deploy your site and assign a custom domain if needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to deploy static sites or JAMstack apps with fast global delivery and simple Git-based workflows.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require server-side rendering, backend APIs, or dynamic server logic as part of your site.
entry.body: Cloudflare Pages is a static site hosting platform with built-in CI/CD, global CDN, and seamless integration with Git providers for fast, secure deployments.

entry.title: Firebase Hosting
entry.description: Firebase Hosting provides fast, secure static and dynamic web hosting with global CDN and SSL, integrated with other Firebase services.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes generous monthly limits for hosting bandwidth and storage.
billingRiskNotes: Overages can incur charges if limits are exceeded.

useCases: Serving web apps with global CDN
quickstartSteps: Install Firebase CLI with npm install -g firebase-tools.
Run firebase login to authenticate with your Google account.
Initialize your project using firebase init hosting.
Build your static site and place files in the public directory.
Deploy with firebase deploy.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need fast, secure static or single-page app hosting with easy deployment and global CDN.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require backend compute, advanced server-side rendering, or need hard caps to prevent overages.
entry.body: Firebase Hosting offers fast, secure, and reliable web hosting for static and dynamic content, with easy integration into the Firebase ecosystem and global CDN.

entry.title: Fly.io
entry.description: Fly.io lets you deploy full-stack apps and databases close to users globally with minimal DevOps overhead.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 3 shared-cpu-1x VMs, 3GB persistent volume, and 160GB outbound data per month.
billingRiskNotes: Hard cap on free resources; overages require payment method.

quickstartSteps: Sign up at https://fly.io/ and install the Fly CLI.
Run 'fly launch' in your project directory to initialize.
Authenticate with 'fly auth login'.
Deploy your app using 'fly deploy'.
Monitor and manage via the Fly dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Fly.io to deploy containerized apps or APIs that need low-latency global access with simple scaling and minimal infrastructure management.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need managed databases in the free tier, require high CPU/RAM, or need compliance certifications for regulated workloads.
entry.body: Fly.io offers a generous always-free tier for deploying containerized applications globally, with simple CLI tools and a developer-friendly experience. The free tier is suitable for small projects, demos, and indie apps, but does not include free managed databases or large persistent storage. Overages are capped unless a payment method is added, reducing billing risk. Fly.io is ideal for developers seeking fast, global deployments with minimal setup.

entry.title: GitHub Pages
entry.description: GitHub Pages offers free static website hosting directly from your GitHub repositories.

freeTierDetails.summary: Host public static sites for free from GitHub repositories.

quickstartSteps: Create a public repository on GitHub.
Add your static site files (HTML, CSS, JS) to the repository.
Commit and push your changes.
Go to the repository Settings > Pages.
Select the branch and folder to publish, then save.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to host static websites or documentation directly from a GitHub repository with minimal setup and no cost.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need dynamic backend features, private site hosting, or advanced server-side processing.
entry.body: GitHub Pages is a free static site hosting service that lets you publish websites directly from your GitHub repositories, ideal for personal, project, or documentation sites.

entry.title: Netlify
entry.description: Netlify offers fast, automated deployment and hosting for static sites and frontend frameworks with integrated CI/CD and serverless functions.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes hosting, continuous deployment, and serverless functions for personal and small projects.
caveats: Custom domains included, but some advanced features require paid plans.
Overages may result in service interruption.
billingRiskNotes: Service may be paused if free limits are exceeded.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Netlify account.
Connect your GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket repository.
Select the repository to deploy and configure build settings.
Deploy your site and access it via the generated Netlify URL.
Optionally, add a custom domain and configure serverless functions.
entry.whenToUse: Use Netlify for fast deployment and hosting of static sites or frontend apps with simple CI/CD and serverless needs.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need backend databases, complex server-side logic, or require advanced enterprise features.
entry.body: Netlify provides a streamlined workflow for deploying static sites and frontend applications, integrating continuous deployment, serverless functions, and custom domains in a user-friendly platform.

entry.title: Netlify Functions
entry.description: Netlify Functions lets you run serverless backend code with automatic deployment and scaling from your Netlify site.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 125,000 requests and 100 hours of execution time per month.
caveats: Hard cap on requests and execution time

useCases: Add serverless APIs to static sites
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Netlify account.
Create or import a site on Netlify.
Add a 'netlify/functions' directory to your project.
Write a JavaScript or TypeScript function and save it in the functions directory.
Deploy your site to Netlify; functions are automatically deployed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to add backend logic or APIs to static sites without managing servers.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need long-running processes, high memory, or custom runtime environments.
entry.body: Netlify Functions provide a simple way to add serverless backend logic to your Netlify-hosted sites, with generous free tier limits and easy deployment.

entry.title: Railway
entry.description: Railway is a developer platform for deploying, hosting, and managing web apps and databases with minimal configuration.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes $5/month of usage for hosting apps and databases.
caveats: Projects are paused when credit is exhausted until next reset.
billingRiskNotes: No charges unless you upgrade to a paid plan.

quickstartSteps: Sign up at railway.app with your GitHub account.
Create a new project and connect your GitHub repository.
Configure environment variables if needed.
Deploy your app with one click or via CLI.
Access your app's live URL and monitor logs.
entry.whenToUse: Use Railway to quickly deploy web apps or APIs without managing infrastructure, especially for prototypes, demos, or small production workloads.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need persistent custom domains on the free tier, require advanced networking, or have workloads exceeding $5/month usage.
entry.body: Railway offers a simple platform for deploying and managing web apps and databases, ideal for rapid prototyping and small-scale production use.

entry.title: Render
entry.description: Render provides managed cloud hosting for web apps, static sites, databases, and background workers with simple deployment workflows.

freeTierDetails.summary: Always-free tier for static sites, web services, and PostgreSQL with usage limits.
caveats: Free services sleep after 15 minutes of inactivity

quickstartSteps: Sign up at render.com using your GitHub or GitLab account.
Create a new service and connect your repository.
Select the type of service (web, static site, database, or background worker).
Configure build and start commands as needed.
Deploy and monitor your service from the Render dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Render for quick deployment of web apps, static sites, or databases without managing infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced networking, custom VM configurations, or require strict uptime guarantees for production-critical workloads.
entry.body: Render offers a generous free tier for hosting static sites, web services, databases, and background workers, making it ideal for rapid prototyping and small projects. The platform automates deployments from Git, provides managed SSL, and includes basic monitoring. Free services may sleep when idle, and there are hard usage caps, but no risk of surprise overages. It's best suited for students, indie developers, and startups needing simple, reliable hosting without infrastructure management.

entry.title: Vercel
entry.description: Vercel provides fast, serverless hosting and deployment for static sites and frontend frameworks with global CDN.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes hobby deployments with limited serverless function execution and bandwidth.

quickstartSteps: Sign up at vercel.com with your GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket account.
Import your repository and select the project framework (e.g., Next.js, React).
Configure build settings if needed or accept defaults.
Click 'Deploy' to trigger your first deployment.
Access your live site via the provided Vercel URL.
entry.whenToUse: Use Vercel for quick, zero-config deployment of static sites or frontend frameworks with automatic previews and global CDN.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need backend databases, custom server regions, or advanced serverless features beyond the free tier limits.
entry.body: Vercel offers a generous free tier for static and frontend hosting, ideal for personal, student, and open source projects with easy Git integration and global CDN.

entry.title: Vercel Edge Functions
entry.description: Vercel Edge Functions let you run serverless code at the edge for fast, dynamic web experiences.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1 million Edge Function invocations per month.
caveats: Hard cap on invocations; requests above limit fail.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Vercel account.
Create or import a project (e.g., Next.js).
Add an Edge Function in the /api or /middleware directory.
Deploy your project to Vercel.
Test your Edge Function via the deployed URL.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need low-latency, serverless code execution close to users for dynamic web features or middleware.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need long-running processes, high memory, or heavy compute workloads; not suitable for backend-only APIs.
entry.body: Vercel Edge Functions enable developers to run serverless JavaScript or TypeScript code at the edge, providing fast, dynamic responses for web applications with a generous free tier suitable for most small to medium projects.

entry.title: Vercel Postgres
entry.description: Vercel Postgres offers managed PostgreSQL databases with instant provisioning and seamless integration for Vercel-hosted applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes one development database with limited storage and connections.
caveats: No production SLA on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up or log in to Vercel.
Navigate to the Storage > Postgres section in the dashboard.
Click 'Create Database' and select the free development tier.
Configure database name and region, then create the database.
Copy connection string and use it in your app or Vercel project.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a quick, managed PostgreSQL database for development or prototyping, especially if deploying on Vercel.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for production workloads, high-traffic apps, or if you need advanced PostgreSQL features or custom configurations.
entry.body: Vercel Postgres provides a simple, serverless PostgreSQL experience tightly integrated with the Vercel platform, ideal for rapid development and testing.

entry.title: Vercel Serverless Functions
entry.description: Deploy serverless APIs and backend logic with zero-config on Vercel’s global edge network.

freeTierDetails.summary: 125 hours of serverless function execution per month on the Hobby plan.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Vercel account.
Install Vercel CLI or connect your GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket repository.
Create an `api/` directory in your project and add a JavaScript or TypeScript file.
Write your serverless function code and export a default handler.
Deploy your project with `vercel` CLI or push to your connected repository.
entry.whenToUse: Use for lightweight APIs, webhooks, or backend logic that benefit from global edge deployment and simple scaling.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for long-running processes, heavy compute workloads, or when you need fine-grained control over infrastructure.
entry.body: Vercel Serverless Functions let you deploy backend logic and APIs with minimal configuration, scaling automatically and running close to your users on Vercel’s edge network.
